Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". 300	func (c *Controller) validateCallbackSecret(ctx context.Context) error {
301		if c.callbackSecret == "" {
302			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "callback secret is not configured")
303		}
304	
305		md, ok := metadata.FromIncomingContext(ctx)
306		if !ok {
307			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "missing metadata")
308		}
309	
310		values := md.Get(callbackSecretHeaderKey)
311		if len(values) != 1 || !hmac.Equal([]byte(values[0]), []byte(c.callbackSecret)) {
312			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, "invalid callback secret")
313		}
314	
315		return nil
316	}

318	func toGRPCError(ctx context.Context, method string, err error) error {
319		switch {
320		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrNotFound):
321			return status.Error(codes.NotFound, err.Error())
322		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrBadRequest):
323			return status.Error(codes.InvalidArgument, err.Error())
324		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrFailedValidation):
325			return status.Error(codes.FailedPrecondition, err.Error())
326		case errors.Is(err, terrors.ErrUnauthenticated):
327			return status.Error(codes.Unauthenticated, err.Error())
328		default:
329			tlog.Error(ctx, "%s failed: %v", method, err)
330			return status.Error(codes.Internal, "internal server error")
331		}
332	}

334	func contractToProto(contract *models.Contract) *handset.GetActiveContractForServiceResponse {

340			},
341		}
342	}

344	func contractToProduct(contract *models.Contract, h *models.Handset) *handset.ContractProduct {
345		name := contract.HandsetName
346		basePrice := parsePenceOrZero(contract.RetailPrice)
347		monthlyRepayment := parsePenceOrZero(contract.ContractPrice)
348	
349		if h != nil {
350			if name == "" {
351				name = h.CurrentProperties.Name
352			}
353			if basePrice == 0 {
354				basePrice = h.CurrentProperties.RRPPence
355			}
356			if monthlyRepayment == 0 {
357				monthlyRepayment = h.CurrentProperties.RentalPricePence
358			}
359		}
360	
361		// Avoid int->int32 wraparound on unexpected/corrupted data.
362		basePrice32 := int32(0)
363		if basePrice >= math.MinInt32 && basePrice <= math.MaxInt32 {
364			basePrice32 = int32(basePrice) // nolint: gosec
365		}
366		monthlyRepayment32 := int32(0)
367		if monthlyRepayment >= math.MinInt32 && monthlyRepayment <= math.MaxInt32 {
368			monthlyRepayment32 = int32(monthlyRepayment) // nolint: gosec
369		}
370	
371		return &handset.ContractProduct{
372			Id:               contract.HandsetID,
373			Name:             name,
374			BasePrice:        basePrice32,
375			MonthlyRepayment: monthlyRepayment32,
376		}
377	}
378	
379	func parsePenceOrZero(s string) int {
380		if s == "" {
381			return 0
382		}
383		v, err := strconv.Atoi(s)
384		if err != nil {
385			return 0
386		}
387		return v
388	}

390	func recordRequestMetric(method string, err error) {
391		if err != nil {
392			requestCounter.WithLabelValues(method, "error").Inc()
393		} else {
394			requestCounter.WithLabelValues(method, "ok").Inc()
395		}
396	}

398	func toContractTerm(h models.HandsetProperties) (int32, error) {
399		i := h.ContractTermMonths
400		if i < math.MinInt32 || i > math.MaxInt32 {
401			return 0, fmt.Errorf("contract term value %d overflows int32", i)
402		}
403		return int32(i), nil // nolint: gosec
404	}

406	func handsetToProto(h models.Handset) (*handset.Handset, error) {
407		term, err := toContractTerm(h.CurrentProperties)

410		}
411	
412		p := h.CurrentProperties
413		return &handset.Handset{
414			Id:                 h.ID.String(),
415			Name:               p.Name,
416			RentalPrice:        toMoney(p.RentalPricePence),
417			Rrp:                toMoney(p.RRPPence),
418			ContractTermMonths: term,
419			Manufacturer:       p.Manufacturer,
420			Model:              p.Model,
421			Colours:            p.Colours,
422			Capacity:           p.Capacity,
423			ScreenSize:         p.ScreenSize,
424			Battery:            p.Battery,
425			SdCapacity:         p.SDCapacity,
426			WifiStandard:       p.WiFiStandard,
427			Esim:               p.ESim,
428			DualSim:            p.DualSim,
429			SaleStatus:         p.SaleStatus,
430			IsNew:              p.IsNew,
431			SpecificationsLink: p.SpecificationsLink,
432		}, nil
433	}

436		if ts == nil {

**`service.go`** — Three conflicts:

// checkPreEligibility perform pre-eligibility checks
func (s Service) checkPreEligibility(ctx context.Context, customerID string) error {
	// Check for declined contract
	hasDeclined, err := s.store.CustomerHasDeclinedContract(ctx, customerID, s.backoffPeriod)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to check declined contract: %w", err)
	}
	if hasDeclined {
		return fmt.Errorf("a declined contract exists within the backup period: %w", terrors.ErrFailedValidation)
	}

	// Customer is eligible
	return nil
}

// EligibilityCallbackFailure handles a failure callback from the eligibility provider,
// indicating the eligibility process could not be completed (e.g. KYC or credit check failure).
func (s *Service) EligibilityCallbackFailure(ctx context.Context, requestID string, outcome models.EligibilityFailureOutcome) error {
	if requestID == "" {
		return fmt.Errorf("request_id is required: %w", terrors.ErrBadRequest)
	}

	contract, err := s.store.GetContractByRequestID(ctx, requestID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get contract: %w", err)
	}

	if contract.Status != models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility {
		tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
			WithAttribute("current_status", string(contract.Status)).
			Info(ctx, "eligibility failure callback ignored, contract is not pending eligibility")
		return nil
	}

	if err := s.store.UpdateContractEligibilityFailed(ctx, requestID, outcome); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update contract status: %w", err)
	}

	if err := s.eventProducer.ProduceMobileHandsetContractDeclinedSync(ctx, &serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractDeclined{
		ServiceId:  contract.ServiceID,
		CustomerId: contract.CustomerID,
		ContractId: contract.ID.String(),
		HandsetId:  contract.HandsetID,
	}); err != nil {
		tlog.WithError(err).
			WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
			Error(ctx, "failed to produce MobileHandsetContractDeclined event")
	}

	tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
		WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
		Info(ctx, "eligibility failed, contract declined")

	return nil
}

// EligibilityStatusUpdate handles status update notifications from the eligibility provider.
// For DECLINED and WITHDRAWN statuses, the contract is moved to DECLINED.
func (s *Service) EligibilityStatusUpdate(ctx context.Context, requestID, statusStr string) error {
	// @todo matt - this is a bit of a catch-all for any future status updates from the eligibility provider that require action. we don't really know what the statuses in the proto actually represent at this stage, neither do we know which are terminal states and how they should interact with the success/failure callbacks. consider this method WIP for the time being.
	if requestID == "" {
		return fmt.Errorf("request_id is required: %w", terrors.ErrBadRequest)
	}

	if statusStr != "DECLINED" && statusStr != "WITHDRAWN" {
		tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
			WithAttribute("status", statusStr).
			Info(ctx, "eligibility status update received, no action required")
		return nil
	}

	contract, err := s.store.GetContractByRequestID(ctx, requestID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get contract: %w", err)
	}

	if contract.Status != models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility {
		tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
			WithAttribute("current_status", string(contract.Status)).
			Info(ctx, "eligibility status update ignored, contract is not pending eligibility")
		return nil
	}

	if err := s.store.UpdateContractStatus(ctx, requestID, models.ContractStatusDeclined); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update contract status: %w", err)
	}

	if err := s.eventProducer.ProduceMobileHandsetContractDeclinedSync(ctx, &serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractDeclined{
		ServiceId:  contract.ServiceID,
		CustomerId: contract.CustomerID,
		ContractId: contract.ID.String(),
		HandsetId:  contract.HandsetID,
	}); err != nil {
		tlog.WithError(err).
			WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
			Error(ctx, "failed to produce MobileHandsetContractDeclined event")
	}

	tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
		WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
		WithAttribute("status", statusStr).
		Info(ctx, "eligibility status update, contract declined")

	return nil
}

// EligibilityStatusUpdate handles status update notifications from the eligibility provider.
// For DECLINED and WITHDRAWN statuses, the contract is moved to DECLINED.
func (s *Service) EligibilityStatusUpdate(ctx context.Context, requestID, statusStr string) error {
	// @todo matt - this is a bit of a catch-all for any future status updates from the eligibility provider that require action. we don't really know what the statuses in the proto actually represent at this stage, neither do we know which are terminal states and how they should interact with the success/failure callbacks. consider this method WIP for the time being.
	if requestID == "" {
		return fmt.Errorf("request_id is required: %w", terrors.ErrBadRequest)
	}

	if statusStr != "DECLINED" && statusStr != "WITHDRAWN" {
		tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
			WithAttribute("status", statusStr).
			Info(ctx, "eligibility status update received, no action required")
		return nil
	}

	contract, err := s.store.GetContractByRequestID(ctx, requestID)
	if err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to get contract: %w", err)
	}

	if contract.Status != models.ContractStatusPendingEligibility {
		tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
			WithAttribute("current_status", string(contract.Status)).
			Info(ctx, "eligibility status update ignored, contract is not pending eligibility")
		return nil
	}

	if err := s.store.UpdateContractStatus(ctx, requestID, models.ContractStatusDeclined); err != nil {
		return fmt.Errorf("failed to update contract status: %w", err)
	}

	if err := s.eventProducer.ProduceMobileHandsetContractDeclinedSync(ctx, &serviceevents.MobileHandsetContractDeclined{
		ServiceId:  contract.ServiceID,
		CustomerId: contract.CustomerID,
		ContractId: contract.ID.String(),
		HandsetId:  contract.HandsetID,
	}); err != nil {
		tlog.WithError(err).
			WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
			Error(ctx, "failed to produce MobileHandsetContractDeclined event")
	}

	tlog.WithAttribute("request_id", requestID).
		WithAttribute("contract_id", contract.ID.String()).
		WithAttribute("status", statusStr).
		Info(ctx, "eligibility status update, contract declined")

	return nil
}
